pattern: Distance decomposes per bit position: for a given bit, the only pairs that differ are those pairing a number with the bit set against one without, so each bit contributes (count of ones) times (count of zeros).
hints: Summing the distance for every pair directly is quadratic and too slow for large arrays. Each bit position can be considered independently of the others.
Fix one bit column. Among the n numbers, suppose c of them have that bit set; the remaining n - c do not. How many of the n*(n-1)/2 pairs differ in this column?
A pair differs at a bit exactly when one has it set and the other does not, giving c*(n-c) differing pairs per bit. Sum that over all bit positions (up to ~30).

edge_inputs.description: three values, every pair differs in two bits
a repeated value reduces the differing pairs
single element has no pairs, distance zero
two zeros, smallest possible values, distance zero

stress.description: many random values across the full value range
stress.note: large arrays exercise the linear per-bit aggregation versus the quadratic oracle
